items.title: Trump says there is ‘nothing wrong’ with family’s crypto windfall
items.description: The president reported at least $1.4 billion in crypto income for 2025. He told CNBC he did nothing illegal and was not aware of the extent of his holdings. Critics say he is profiting from the office while his administration writes the industry's rules.

items.title: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion of bitcoin in 2 weeks even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
items.description: U.S. institutional demand had its worst month ever in June. Large holders absorbed the selling, marking a divergence that has shown up near past cycle bottoms.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether traders aren't fully buying the bounce, options markets show
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 3, 2026

items.title: Crypto bulls on firmer footing as U.S. rate-hike risk recedes
items.description: Crypto ended the week in a more buoyant state after weak U.S. jobs data reduced the chances of a Fed interest-rate increase and Uniswap gained on a link-up with Robinhood.

items.title: Memory and semiconductor stocks lose momentum, bitcoin rebounds in sign of changing investor focus
items.description: After dominating markets in 2026, AI-tied memory and semiconductor stocks are losing momentum, raising the question whether capital will shift back into bitcoin.

items.title: Tokenization could make finance faster, but also more susceptible to shocks, IMF says
items.description: Tokenization could make finance faster and cheaper. It also makes it more vulnerable to sudden shocks, the International Monetary Fund said.

items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin rises above $62,000 as the red hot semiconductor trade starts to fade
Binance says MiCA should be judged by who it licenses, not who it excludes
items.description: Europe head Gillian Lynch said Binance met Greece's licensing requirements and that the company remains committed to the EU, despite withdrawing its MiCA application days before the July 1 deadline.

items.title: ESMA warns many prediction market event contracts already face EU retail ban
items.description: The European regulator said companies cannot circumvent EU financial rules by marketing binary-style products as event contracts rather than derivatives.

items.title: Crypto Biz: Bitcoin maximalism meets the realities of capital markets
items.description: Strategy authorizes Bitcoin sales, Open USD takes on USDT and USDC, Fidelity defends Bitcoin security and crypto ramps up political spending for 2026.

items.title: Bitcoin price tags $62.3K nine-day high after global stocks hit historic record
items.description: Bitcoin set new highs for July after the Dow Jones and global stocks market cap set new all-time highs before the US Independence Day holiday.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends
items.description: Lawyers and industry executives expect EU regulators to enforce MiCA differently as unauthorized crypto companies are required to wind down operations.

items.title: Upbit says it only expressed interest in future OUSD participation
items.description: Upbit said it only expressed interest in potentially joining the OpenStandard ecosystem in the future, as several South Korean firms distanced themselves from the OUSD initiative.

items.title: Belgian police arrest suspected phishing gang leader tied to $572K theft
items.description: Belgian authorities say a European phishing gang stole over $572,000 from victims before laundering the proceeds through cryptocurrency.

items.title: StanChart joins ESMA's first MiCA register update since deadline
items.description: ESMA publishes its first post-deadline MiCA register update, adding 37 crypto-asset service providers, including Standard Chartered and FalconX.

items.title: 5 takeaways from President Donald Trump's interview with CNBC
items.description: The president defended his family's business and said he feels bad that the presidency creates a conflict of interest in any of his children's investments.

items.title: Ford achieves quality milestone, as CEO targets flawless new vehicle launches
items.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley told CNBC that the automaker has learned from its past quality and recall issues, which have hurt its earnings and stained its reputation.

items.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
items.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

items.title: Trump’s Freedom 250 draws corporate sponsors with business before his administration
items.description: Companies with federal interests — including Boeing, Lockheed Martin and Oracle — are sponsoring a Trump-aligned effort celebrating America’s 250th birthday.

items.title: As ACA enrollment falls by millions, Trump administration and policy gurus disagree on why
items.description: Affordable Care Act marketplace enrollment has declined by 3 million people. The Trump administration points to fraud controls. Policy experts point to cost.

items.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
items.description: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.

items.title: Gold prices set for first weekly rise in a month as investors scale back Fed rate hike bets
items.description: The metal was boosted as investors scaled back their bets on a looming rate hike.

items.title: Venezuela quake toll climbs to 2,595 as damage estimates mount after strongest tremor in a century
items.description: Death toll from twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela jumped to 2,595, as rescue operations continued after the country's strongest tremors in over a century.

items.title: Bitcoin edges above $62k, set for weekly rise after softer U.S. jobs data
BTSE Group Launches BTSE Indonesia Through Joint Venture, Enters One of Asia’s Fastest-Growing Crypto Markets
Bitcoin wedged below $63,400 resistance: Live levels
Bitcoin rises 2%, briefly tops $62k as weak U.S. jobs data eases rate hike fears
Strategy’s bitcoin trading policy creates risks for broader markets, says JPMorgan
Bybit Card Launches in Peru: Seamless Spending with Up to 120 USDT in Rewards
BTCC Exchange Sees Trading Volume Surge Ahead of Argentina Match Days as World Cup Showdown Campaign Heats Up
Bybit Launches Fiat & Pay Summer Splash Campaign With 25,000 USDT Prize Pool
‘It’s whack-a-mole’: how Europe’s smart border melted down
items.description: Automated system first mooted in 2008 was meant to keep out criminals — instead, it is delaying travellers

items.title: John Roberts, the US chief justice playing the long game
items.description: Under him the Supreme Court has defied Trump, while at the same time expanding the scope of presidential power

items.title: How crowds become stupider
items.description: Prediction markets suffer when we all think the same way

items.title: Albania’s flamingos meet the Trump family bulldozers
items.description: Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump plan a luxury residential complex that locals say will threaten a natural reserve

items.title: Trump will oppose heavy US AI regulation, says outgoing tech adviser
items.description: Sriram Krishnan tells the FT the president is against a centralised regulator as AI backlash grows

items.title: Monaco bomb suspect is a woman from Ukraine
items.description: Prosecutors issue international arrest warrant for woman believed to have fled to Germany

items.title: Surging Wall Street profit forecasts fuel fears of ‘earnings bubble’
items.description: Analysts’ expectations for S&P 500 earnings are rising at fastest rate since rebound from Covid pandemic

items.title: This is nuts upon nuts. When’s the crash?
items.description: An earnings/valuation double bubble of Pleistocene proportions

items.title: The World Order Changes After the World Cup

items.title: The World Cup has made Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha a $17 million social-media star
items.description: As Cape Verde faces off against Lionel Messi and Argentina tonight, here’s a look at what could be next for the breakout World Cup star

items.title: AI infrastructure stocks have overtaken the tech hyperscalers in a shift UBS calls ‘extraordinary’
items.description: A research team at UBS sees value creation in the artificial intelligence infrastructure sector soaring 600% in the space of four years, compared with just 100% for “hyperscalers.”

items.title: A looming AI disappointment and rising yields. Why one research firm is hitting the brakes on U.S. stocks.
items.description: In a market dominated by tech stocks, a big test is facing investors in the next 12 months, warns MacroResearchBoard

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: U.S. stocks have delivered 8.7% a year since independence was declared in 1776
items.description: What 250 years of independence have meant for America’s investors.

items.title: ‘She wants him gone’: My friend took in a homeless man as a caretaker. After 10 years, how can she evict him?
items.description: “He lived rent-free, helping her with health-related issues and other tasks.”

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.description: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”

items.title: I have no kids. Will I cause family drama by leaving different amounts to my nieces and nephews?
items.description: “Part of me would prefer to leave more to those for whom the money could make a meaningful difference.”

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Iran begins mourning for Khamenei under tight security
items.description: Dignitaries from country and neighbouring states pay respects to slain supreme leader ahead of official funeral

items.title: Why US exceptionalism in markets is justified
items.description: An honest conclusion is that America’s centrality rests on a set of mutually reinforcing advantages

items.title: Shell alleged to have misled UK courts in Nigeria pollution case
items.description: Long-running trial over blighting of Niger Delta due to begin next year

items.title: Sitdown in Sintra
items.description: Chair Kevin Warsh gave little away on the direction of Fed rates at the ECB’s annual gathering of central bankers

items.title: Russian shadow tankers avoid English Channel after naval interceptions
items.description: Crackdown by European navies forces sanctioned vessels to take circuitous north Atlantic route around UK

items.title: Top official recuses herself from NHS Palantir contract decision after business links
items.description: MPs had raised concerns over Samantha Jones’s previous consulting work

items.title: Trump doubles down on support for Venezuela’s Delcy Rodríguez
items.description: Washington is tying itself closely to a government that faces public anger over its response to devastating earthquakes

items.title: Forecasting recessions? GLWT
items.description: Alphaville 🤜 🤛 The Economics Show

items.title: Why Some Banks Still Charge High Overdraft Fees
items.description: Congress scrapped a rule last year that capped how much banks could charge if you spent more than was in your account. Now, banks’ revenue from the fees is rising.

items.title: Will Late-Night TV Work on YouTube?
items.description: Julian Shapiro-Barnum, best known for a social media series called “Recess Therapy,” has started an online-only late-night show.

items.title: Why One Global Shipping Choke Point Isn’t a Blueprint for Another
items.description: The Strait of Malacca may be a model for how Oman and Iran could collect fees in the Strait of Hormuz, but the differences between the waterways are vast.

items.title: The Hottest Stock Markets Lead to the Biggest Losses
items.description: Investor enthusiasm culminated in some of the worst cases of wealth destruction in the last 100 years, a long-running study shows.

items.title: Egg Producers to Pay $3.3 Million to Settle Federal Price-Fixing Lawsuit
items.description: The companies also agreed to donate more than 53 million eggs to food banks and nonprofits in 17 states that joined the federal government in the antitrust action.

items.title: Many Children Don’t Have ‘Trump Accounts.’ Some Are Missing Out on $1,000.
items.description: More than six million have been signed up for the investment accounts, which open for contributions July 4, but there are barriers to uptake.

items.title: To Reduce Electrical Grid Strain Amid Heat Wave, Data Centers Are Ordered to Use Backup Power
items.description: As triple-digit temperatures engulf much of the United States, the Trump administration wants grid managers to require the use of backup power that often goes unused.

items.title: What Private Credit Is, and Why Investors Are So Worried About It
items.description: Some say the industry expanded too quickly and extended loans to companies that won’t be able to pay them back.
